name: Diff View
description: Side-by-side or unified diff display for code and text comparison.

split.description: Side-by-side split diff view with original and modified panels, line numbers, and change markers

unified.description: Unified diff format with interleaved additions and deletions in a single scrollable column

inline.description: Inline word-level diff highlighting character changes within individual lines

visual_brief: Code comparison display with two side-by-side panels (split preset) or a single merged panel (unified preset). Each panel shows line numbers in a narrow gutter column with a monospace font. Added lines have a green-tinted background with a '+' marker. Removed lines have a red-tinted background with a '-' marker. Unchanged lines render with no background tint. A header bar above each panel shows the file name and change statistics (additions/deletions count). The inline preset highlights changed characters within a line with a stronger color accent.

responsive.mobile: Defaults to unified diff view — single column with added/removed lines interleaved. Horizontal scroll for long lines. Line numbers use minimal width.
responsive.tablet: Split view available with narrower panes. Synchronized scrolling between panels.
responsive.desktop: Full split view with generous panel widths. Character-level diff highlighting. Expandable context lines.

layout_hints.comparison_role: The pattern should preserve a clear comparison between original and changed states. Headers, gutters, and hunk boundaries should all reinforce that reading model rather than acting like generic code panes.
layout_hints.hunk_focus: Changed lines and inline highlights should dominate visually, while unchanged context remains supportive and easy to collapse or skim past.
layout_hints.mobile_fallback: Unified mode is the canonical narrow-screen fallback. Do not try to force split panes into cramped widths where comparison becomes unreadable.

motion.micro: Active hunks or focused lines may brighten subtly, and copied or expanded hunks may acknowledge interaction with restrained feedback.
motion.transitions: Expanding context, switching between split and unified views, and jumping between hunks should preserve orientation without flashy animation.

keyboard: Keyboard navigation between hunks should keep the currently focused change obvious in both split and unified modes.
announcements: Added, removed, and unchanged lines should be distinguishable semantically and not rely only on color or gutter markers.
